signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Peace Optimism
signals.text: NZD/CAD leads G8 performance as geopolitical de-escalation in the Middle East and falling oil prices ($93.2, -1.98%) punish the CAD laggard while supporting risk-sensitive NZD. The 1.393% spread between the G8 leader and laggard confirms a strong bilateral theme. Despite NZD's 1W outperformance, RBNZ 2.25% vs BoC 2.25% remains carry-neutral (0bp), suggesting the move is driven by commodity-linkage divergence and shifting risk sentiment rather than rate differentials. NZD/USD at 0.5976, USD/CAD at 1.3623.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (long% 23.0%) represents a crowded spec fade against a hawkish RBNZ bias. While recent soft labor data caps tightening expectations, the RBNZ remains in a 'Hike' bias for the 27 May meeting. If the central bank maintains its hawkish stance, the extreme spec short positioning faces a significant unwind risk. NZD/USD at 0.5976 is testing the upper bound of recent ranges as USD funding stress eases (DXY -0.17%).

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows institutional conviction in the AUD carry story (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% — +60bp AUD premium). This diverges sharply from retail sentiment, where 89% of traders are short. This institutional vs retail divergence at an extreme level suggests retail capitulation risk if AUD/USD extends above 0.7260. Peace optimism and falling energy costs further support the AUD/CAD parity target mentioned in recent headlines.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.729 vs norm of -0.130 (z=+1.84σ) indicates a significant regime shift where the Euro is trading as a pure risk-on proxy rather than its historical inverse relationship with equities. Despite the 1W gain (+0.39%), EUR/USD upside is structurally limited by the -175bp carry deficit (ECB 2% vs Fed 3.75%). The pair at 1.1774 remains sensitive to US NFP data which determines if the Fed maintains its 'higher-for-longer' stance.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.37 is supported by the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%). While VIX at 17.5 (normal volatility) keeps carry trades attractive, the 160.00 level remains the critical watch zone for BoJ verbal warnings or physical intervention. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) reflects a crowded carry trade that is vulnerable to rapid unwinds only if VIX spikes above 25 or the 16 June BoJ meeting delivers a hawkish surprise.
